_comment: Explicit binding file for the DFlash Allium <-> TLA+ synergy contract.
tla_helpers       — TLA+ symbols that are intentionally NOT invariants:
functions, symmetry sets. These are whitelisted
so the reverse-direction drift check skips them.
divergences       — pairs where a TLA+ INVARIANT must have a different
name from its source Allium invariant. Each entry
records the Allium name, the TLA+ name, and a
rationale. Should stay empty under normal use; the
naming convention prefers identical names. Adding
bindings_external — Allium invariants that are intentionally NOT
bound by a TLA+ invariant. Either bound by a C++
load-time test, an empirical Gate, or out-of-scope.
Recording them here suppresses the forward-drift
check for that name, with rationale.

bindings_external.rationale: The combine_features unit test loads DFLASH_FC weight from the drafter GGUF, runs both fused kernel and scalar reference, and byte-compares context_states output. Reversing FC and hidden_norm or omitting fc produces visibly different output (cosine << 1.0 vs 1.0 byte-identity).
Test parameter assertion: source_hiddens.shape[-1] == 5120 == drafter.hidden_size. Kernel signature enforces D_d=5120 at compile time; runtime shape check at test setup.

Test uses a non-identity hidden_norm_weight (random fp16) so that reversing FC and hidden_norm order produces output that fails byte-identity vs the scalar reference. Float arithmetic property; not TLA+-modelable.
Test allocates context_states at shape [N_slots, MAL_anchors, 5120] without per-layer replication. Inject test's 5 launches all read the same buffer at the same (slot, anchor) coordinate.

Test host-side launcher runs exactly L_d=5 inject kernel launches, one per drafter layer, each reading that layer's k_weight/v_weight/k_norm_weight slice. Loop count asserted; per-layer cache writes verified.

Test loads real per-layer k_weight [1024, 5120] (= 8 heads × 128 dim per head), v_weight same, k_norm_weight [128] from the drafter GGUF. Scalar reference uses the same shapes; fused kernel must match byte-identically.

Scalar oracle applies k_norm + RoPE to K only; V is projected and written without normalization or rotation. Fused kernel must agree byte-identically, so any symmetry violation (e.g., normalizing V) breaks the test. Float arithmetic + structural; not TLA+-modelable.
Test passes anchor_positions[slot, anchor] explicitly; verifies fused kernel writes to k_cache_layer[slot, anchor_pos, :, :] and v_cache_layer[slot, anchor_pos, :, :], not at any other position.

Host launcher is a for-loop over L_d=5 with grid (N_slots, MAL_anchors) per launch. Test exercises this exact structure (no batched 3D launch alternative). Additional perf check at Gate 6 measures L2 hit rate to detect a future regression to inner-loop layer dispatch.

Structural precondition. Multi-layer binding: (a) byte-identical fused-vs-scalar sweeps at T3 catch local determinism violations; (b) np-invariance test at T7 catches batch-shape sensitivity; (c) static grep gate at build time forbids atomicAdd<float>, Split-K patterns, and non-compile-time tile dims in ggml-cuda/dflash-*.cu. No TLA+ operational form exists for this kind of structural rule on float kernels.

Structural: T4 drafter forward is a single cooperative kernel launch per (slot, anchor) cycle producing all (1+BLOCK_SIZE) positions in one pass. Test asserts launch count = 1 per cycle and verifies output covers BLOCK_SIZE candidate tokens. Multi-step denoise schedules are out of contract scope per the same invariant — review-bound.

Test parameter shape assertion: input_tokens.shape == [N_slots, 1 + BLOCK_SIZE]. Anchor at position 0, mask-token positions at 1..BLOCK_SIZE. Output_logits.shape == [N_slots, BLOCK_SIZE, V]. Compile-time enforced via kernel signature; runtime asserted in test setup.

Each of the 5 layer iterations inside dflash_drafter_forward reads from k_cache_layer[L] and v_cache_layer[L]. Test pre-populates all 5 layers' KV cache via dflash_inject_kv_fused and verifies that zeroing any one layer's cache produces detectable change in output_logits. Structural (kernel layer loop body) + empirical (zero-layer ablation).

K-loop iteration bounded by sliding window for layers 0-3 (SWA, window=2048) and unbounded for layer 4 (full attention). No mask materialization. Layer-type read from drafter GGUF metadata at load time. Test exercises a SeqLen > swa_window scenario where SWA bound matters and confirms output differs from full-attention-everywhere. Structural; not TLA+-modelable as a single mask predicate.

Drafter forward kernel reads anchor embedding from target's token_embd.weight (F16). Test materializes shared embedding via the drafter loader path then mutates one row and confirms drafter forward output changes for that token id — proves the drafter is reading the target's embedding tensor, not a private copy. Pointer-equality test in load-time test confirms tensor-handle sharing.

Test passes slot_positions[slot] = anchor_pos. Verifies drafter output's effective anchor position via SWA window bounds: position-conditioning shows up in RoPE phase and K-loop bound for SWA layers. anchor_pos value flows through the kernel without mutation.

BLOCK_SIZE is a kernel template parameter. T4 instantiates {4,5,6,8}; drafter wrapper picks at dispatch from drafter GGUF dflash.block_size metadata. Test sweeps all four BLOCK_SIZE values; each instantiation produces exactly BLOCK_SIZE output positions. Mismatch between requested and template-instantiated BLOCK_SIZE is a build-time failure (no matching template).

Three-run byte-identity sweep: same (weights, anchor, injected, slot_pos, BLOCK_SIZE) inputs across three drafter-forward launches must produce byte-identical output_logits. Validated at T4 unit test + T7 np-invariance test (cross-slot determinism). WMMA-mimicking scalar oracle provides per-run byte-identity binding.

argmax_match kernel computes n_accepted = max k in [0, BLOCK_SIZE] such that drafter argmax matches target argmax for all i < k. Test constructs synthetic logits with planted match/mismatch patterns and asserts n_accepted matches the planted boundary. Ties broken by lowest token id — test exercises tie cases.
Range assertion: 0 <= n_accepted <= BLOCK_SIZE. Test verifies kernel-emitted n_accepted is in range for full-accept, zero-accept, and partial-accept synthetic logit patterns.

bonus_token = argmax(target_logits[n_accepted, :]) for n_accepted < BLOCK_SIZE; for n_accepted == BLOCK_SIZE the bonus is at row BLOCK_SIZE (the extra post-block row). Test plants known argmax values at each row and verifies bonus selection matches the planted target_argmax at row n_accepted.
Test passes anchor_pos and asserts emitted bonus_pos == anchor_pos + n_accepted + 1. Pure positional bookkeeping; not a float-arithmetic check.

argmax_match is a pure function of (DraftBlock, VerifyLogits). Test runs three kernel invocations with identical inputs and asserts byte-identical AcceptDecision output (n_accepted + bonus_token + bonus_pos).

Greedy-only at contract boundary. T4 argmax_match kernel implements argmax_match only; no probabilistic path exists. llama_set_dflash() rejects non-greedy sampler parameters. Test confirms argmax_match is the only entry point and that calling with probabilistic mode flags a hard error (LLAMA_DFLASH_PROBABILISTIC_OUT_OF_SCOPE).

Load-time test: load production target GGUF and drafter GGUF in the same context; assert drafter.token_embd_handle == target.token_embd_handle (pointer equality), drafter.output_handle == target.output_handle. Then mutate a target token_embd row at runtime and confirm drafter sees the change on its next forward (proves shared memory, not just copied values). Wastes ~5 GiB BF16 if not shared per @SharedEmbedAndLMHead — material correctness gate.

target_layer_ids are read once from drafter GGUF metadata at llama_set_dflash() time and stored as a compile-time-derivable constant array per the DFlash context. Test verifies that runtime mutation of the stored target_layer_ids produces no effect on drafter forward output for the current context (the kernel's loop bound is locked at init). Mid-session changes hard-blocked at the C API boundary.
